seoTitle: MacMic Products Distributor | IGBT Module, FRED, Power Discrete | LiTong
seoDescription: Browse MacMic product categories: IGBT modules (HN high-speed, 6TC cost-effective series), FRED chips and modules, power discrete devices. In stock, competitive pricing, technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by MacMic?
faqs.answer: MacMic offers four core product categories: (1) IGBT Modules - Featuring HN high-speed series (up to 50KHz switching) and 6TC cost-effective series for motor drives and EVs; (2) FRED Chips & Modules - Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diodes with low switching losses and soft recovery; (3) Power Discrete Devices - IGBT and FRED in TO-220, TO-3P, and TO-247 packages; (4) MOSFET Modules - Low on-resistance modules for synchronous rectification. All products use advanced Trench Field-Stop technology.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: How do I choose between HN series and 6TC series IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Choose HN series for high-frequency applications requiring switching speeds up to 50KHz, such as welding machines, induction heating, and high-performance motor drives. HN series offers faster switching with Vce(sat) around 2.0V. Choose 6TC series for cost-sensitive motor drive applications where switching frequency is typically 5-15KHz. 6TC series provides excellent cost-performance ratio with Vce(sat) around 1.8V, making it ideal for general-purpose industrial inverters and EV applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our IGBT modules category to compare HN and 6TC series specifications and find the optimal solution for your switching frequency requirements.

faqs.question: What voltage and current ratings are available for MacMic IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: MacMic IGBT modules cover a wide range of voltage and current ratings: Voltage ratings include 600V, 650V, 1200V, and 1700V to suit different application requirements. Current ratings range from 6A to 820A, with popular modules including 15A, 30A, 50A, 75A, 100A, 150A, 200A, 300A, 400A, 600A, and 800A. This comprehensive range supports applications from small servo drives to large industrial inverters and EV powertrains.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore the IGBT modules category to find detailed specifications for each voltage and current rating combination.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: MacMic IGBT modules feature advanced Trench Field-Stop technology with HN high-speed series supporting up to 50KHz switching and 6TC cost-effective series optimized for motor drives. Available from 600V to 1700V and 6A to 820A, these modules deliver excellent performance for industrial and EV applications.
categories.longDescription: MacMic's IGBT module portfolio includes the innovative HN high-speed series enabling designs up to 50KHz in hard switching applications, and the cost-effective 6TC series optimized for motor drive applications. All modules adopt the latest Trench Field-Stop chip technology, providing low conduction losses and excellent switching characteristics. The product range covers voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V and current ratings from 6A to 820A, supporting applications from small servo drives to large industrial inverters and electric vehicle powertrains. MacMic also offers specialized EV series and 3-level series for advanced power conversion topologies. As your trusted MacMic IGBT distributor, we provide comprehensive technical support and competitive pricing. Looking for MacMic IGBT Selection guidance? Check out our selection guide below. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBT Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select the Right MacMic IGBT Module
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to choosing between HN high-speed series and 6TC cost-effective series based on switching frequency and application requirements
keyConsiderations: Switching frequency: HN series for >20KHz, 6TC series for 5-15KHz
Voltage rating: 600V/650V for 400V systems, 1200V for 690V systems
Current rating: Include 1.5-2x safety margin for overload conditions
Package type: Standard packages for easy replacement
Cost target: 6TC series for cost-sensitive applications

faqs.question: How do I choose between HN series and 6TC series IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: HN series is designed for high-frequency applications up to 50KHz, such as welding machines and induction heating, with Vce(sat) around 2.0V. 6TC series is optimized for cost-sensitive motor drive applications with switching frequencies of 5-15KHz, offering excellent cost-performance ratio with Vce(sat) around 1.8V. Choose HN for high-frequency, 6TC for cost-sensitive motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check our IGBT selection guide for detailed comparison and application recommendations.

faqs.question: What voltage rating should I choose for my application?
faqs.answer: For 400V AC input systems (rectified to ~565V DC), use 600V or 650V rated modules. For 690V AC industrial systems (rectified to ~975V DC), use 1200V rated modules. Always include at least 15-20% voltage margin for reliable operation under transient conditions. Contact our FAE team for specific application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our IGBT modules to find the right voltage rating for your system.

faqs.question: How do I determine the right current rating for my motor drive?
faqs.answer: Size current rating based on your motor power and overload requirements. For continuous operation, select a module with current rating 1.3-1.5x your maximum RMS current. For applications with overload requirements, use 1.5-2x margin. Consider thermal limitations - actual current capability depends on heat sink performance. Our FAE team can help with detailed thermal calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our IGBT module specifications or contact FAE for current rating assistance.

products.shortDescription: MacMic MMG75HB060H1A 600V 75A high-speed IGBT module. HN series supports up to 50KHz switching for welding and induct...
descriptionParagraphs: The MacMic MMG75HB060H1A is a high-performance 600V 75A IGBT module from the HN series, featuring advanced Trench Field-Stop technology for high-frequency switching applications up to 50KHz.
With Vce(sat) of 2.0V typical and excellent switching characteristics, this H1A package module delivers reliable performance in demanding welding and induction heating applications.
The compact H1A package ensures compatibility with industry-standard designs, while the high-speed switching capability enables compact magnetic component design and improved system efficiency.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMG75HB060H1A is a high-speed IGBT module designed for applications requiring fast switching frequencies. Utilizing advanced Trench Field-Stop technology, this module can operate at switching frequencies up to 50KHz in hard switching applications, making it ideal for welding machines, induction heating equipment, and high-performance motor drives. The 600V voltage rating is well-suited for 400V AC input systems, while the 75A current rating supports medium-power applications. The module features low conduction losses with typical Vce(sat) of 2.0V and excellent switching characteristics that minimize switching losses at high frequencies. The H1A package provides compact size with good thermal performance and is compatible with standard mounting configurations.
features: Trench Field-Stop technology for high-speed switching
Switching frequency up to 50KHz in hard switching
Low conduction losses with Vce(sat) = 2.0V typical
75A current rating with good overload capability
Compact H1A package with standard mounting

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my 10 years supporting welding and induction heating applications, the MMG75HB060H1A has consistently delivered excellent high-frequency performance. The 50KHz switching capability is genuine - I've verified this in multiple welding machine designs running at 40-50KHz without issues. The key advantage is the ability to use smaller magnetic components, reducing overall system size and cost. One practical tip: ensure proper gate drive design with appropriate gate resistors (I typically start with 10-15Ω for high-frequency applications) to optimize switching performance. Thermal management is also critical at high frequencies - I recommend using thermal interface material with at least 3 W/mK thermal conductivity and maintaining case temperature below 80°C for long-term reliability.
faeReview.highlight: Genuine 50KHz switching capability enables compact magnetic design for welding and induction heating

comparison.compatibility: Pin-compatible, direct replacement with higher current

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating (100A vs 75A) for applications requiring 33% more power handling in the same H1A package with identical switching performance
6TC series with 10% lower Vce(sat) for cost-sensitive applications where 20KHz switching is sufficient

faqs.question: What are the main application scenarios for MMG75HB060H1A?
faqs.answer: MMG75HB060H1A is optimized for high-power switching applications. Its 600V voltage rating and 75A current capability make it ideal for motor drives, inverters, power supplies, and renewable energy systems. The H1A package provides excellent thermal performance for continuous operation. Typical applications include: industrial motor drives, solar inverters, UPS systems, welding equipment, and induction heating systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to evaluate MMG75HB060H1A for your power electronics design and receive thermal design recommendations.

faqs.question: How does MMG75HB060H1A compare to competing IGBTs?
faqs.answer: MMG75HB060H1A offers competitive performance in its voltage and current class. The device features low VCE(sat) for reduced conduction losses and optimized switching characteristics for efficient high-frequency operation. Compared to standard devices, it provides better thermal performance and reliability. MacMic Science & Technology's manufacturing consistency ensures tight parameter distribution, which is critical for parallel operation. The integrated features and robust design make it suitable for demanding industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a detailed comparison including efficiency analysis and thermal calculations for your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for MMG75HB060H1A?
faqs.answer: For optimal MMG75HB060H1A performance: (1) Thermal management - ensure adequate heatsink mounting with thermal interface material. Use proper thermal vias and copper areas for heat spreading. (2) Gate drive - keep gate traces short to minimize inductance. Use appropriate gate resistor values to control switching speed. (3) Kelvin connection - use separate sense connections for accurate current measurement. (4) Layout symmetry - maintain symmetrical layout in multi-device configurations. (5) Snubber circuits - consider RC snubbers to suppress voltage spikes from parasitic inductance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our power electronics layout guide or contact our FAE team for PCB layout review and optimization support.

faqs.question: What are the recommended operating conditions for MMG75HB060H1A?
faqs.answer: MMG75HB060H1A operates as a 600V IGBT with continuous collector current up to 75A at rated temperature. The gate threshold voltage is typically 4-6V, with recommended gate drive voltage of 15V for full enhancement. The maximum junction temperature is 150°C, but for reliable long-term operation, maintain Tj below 125°C. VCE(sat) increases with temperature, so consider thermal derating in your design. The maximum pulsed current is typically 2-4 times the continuous rating. Always include safety margins in your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete datasheet for detailed electrical characteristics or contact our FAE team for thermal analysis and derating curves.

faqs.question: What are common design issues with MMG75HB060H1A and their solutions?
faqs.answer: Common MMG75HB060H1A design challenges: (1) Excessive switching losses - caused by slow gate drive or inadequate gate voltage. Solution: Use gate driver with sufficient peak current, ensure proper gate drive voltage. (2) Voltage spikes during turn-off - due to parasitic inductance. Solution: Minimize loop inductance, add snubber circuits if necessary. (3) Thermal issues - caused by insufficient heatsinking. Solution: Use adequate heatsink, apply proper thermal interface material. (4) Gate oscillations - due to long gate traces or inadequate damping. Solution: Keep gate traces short, add appropriate gate resistor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for design review services or access our application notes library.

faqs.question: How to calculate power losses and select heatsink for MMG75HB060H1A?
faqs.answer: To calculate MMG75HB060H1A power losses: Conduction loss = VCE(sat) × IC × duty cycle. Switching loss depends on switching frequency and gate drive conditions. Total loss = conduction loss + switching loss. For heatsink selection: Required Rth = (Tjmax - Ta) / Ploss - RthJC - RthCS. Select heatsink with adequate thermal resistance and consider airflow conditions. For natural convection, choose larger heatsink with lower thermal resistance. Always include safety margins in thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed loss calculations and thermal design recommendations for your specific application.

With Vce(sat) of 1.8V typical and excellent cost-performance ratio, this T1A package module delivers reliable performance in industrial motor drives and electric vehicle powertrains.
The industry-standard T1A package ensures compatibility with existing designs, while the 1200V rating supports 690V AC systems commonly used in industrial applications.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMG200HB120T1A is a cost-effective IGBT module from the 6TC series, specifically designed for motor drive and electric vehicle applications. Utilizing advanced Trench Field-Stop technology, this module offers an excellent balance between performance and cost. The 1200V voltage rating is ideal for 690V AC industrial systems and EV powertrains, while the 200A current rating supports medium to high-power applications. With typical Vce(sat) of 1.8V, the module provides low conduction losses for improved system efficiency. The T1A package is an industry-standard form factor that ensures compatibility with existing designs and wide availability of compatible heat sinks. This module is particularly well-suited for variable frequency drives, servo systems, and EV motor controllers where cost-effectiveness is important.
features: Trench Field-Stop technology for optimal performance
Low conduction losses with Vce(sat) = 1.8V typical
Standard T1A package for easy integration

faeReview.author: Michael Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Drives
faeReview.content: In my 12 years supporting motor drive applications, the MMG200HB120T1A has become one of my go-to recommendations for cost-sensitive projects. The cost-performance ratio is excellent - you get 1200V 200A capability at a very competitive price point. I've used this module in numerous VFD designs from 30kW to 75kW with great results. The 1.8V Vce(sat) is genuinely achievable and makes a noticeable difference in efficiency compared to older generation IGBTs. One key recommendation: pay attention to the switching characteristics at your operating frequency - while rated for 20KHz, I find the sweet spot is around 8-12KHz for optimal balance of efficiency and EMI. Also, the T1A package is very forgiving thermally if you design your heat sink properly.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent cost-performance ratio for motor drives with genuine 1.8V Vce(sat) performance

categories.name: FRED Modules
categories.description: MacMic Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode (FRED) modules feature low switching losses and soft recovery characteristics. Available in various voltage and current ratings for rectifier and freewheeling applications.
categories.longDescription: MacMic FRED (Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode) modules are designed for high-frequency rectification and freewheeling applications. These modules feature low reverse recovery charge (Qrr) and soft recovery characteristics, minimizing switching losses and EMI in power conversion systems. The product range covers voltage ratings from 200V to 1200V and current ratings from 15A to 400A, supporting applications such as PFC boost stages, output rectifiers, brake choppers, and anti-parallel diodes for IGBT modules. MacMic FRED modules use advanced epitaxial technology to achieve fast recovery times while maintaining low forward voltage drop. As your trusted MacMic FRED distributor, we offer expert guidance on selecting the right FRED for your application. Need MacMic FRED Selection assistance? Check our selection guide below. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive FRED Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuideLink.description: View SelectionGuide,  Voltage, Current RestoreFeature FRED .

categories.seoKeywords: MacMic FRED, fast recovery diode, FRED module, power diode, rectifier module, MacMic distributor

selectionGuide.title: How to Select the Right MacMic FRED Module
selectionGuide.description: Guide to choosing FRED modules based on voltage, current, and recovery characteristics for rectification applications
keyConsiderations: Voltage rating: 1.5-2x maximum reverse voltage
Current rating: Include margin for peak currents
Recovery time: Faster for higher frequency applications
Qrr: Lower for reduced switching losses
Package: Match to IGBT module for anti-parallel use

faqs.question: What is the difference between 600V and 1200V FRED modules?
faqs.answer: 600V FRED modules are designed for 400V AC input systems (rectified to ~565V DC) and offer faster recovery times (35-40ns). 1200V FRED modules are for 690V AC industrial systems (rectified to ~975V DC) with recovery times of 50-55ns. Choose 600V for standard industrial drives, 1200V for high-voltage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check your DC bus voltage and select FRED with 1.5-2x voltage margin.

faqs.question: How does recovery time affect my application?
faqs.answer: Recovery time (trr) determines switching losses in high-frequency applications. Faster recovery (35ns) reduces switching losses at frequencies above 50KHz, ideal for PFC and high-frequency inverters. Standard recovery (50ns) is sufficient for motor drives operating at 5-15KHz. Soft recovery characteristic also affects EMI - MacMic FREDs offer soft recovery to minimize voltage overshoot.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider your switching frequency when selecting recovery time.

faqs.question: Can I use MacMic FRED modules as anti-parallel diodes for IGBTs?
faqs.answer: Yes, MacMic FRED modules are specifically designed for anti-parallel diode applications. Match the FRED voltage rating to your IGBT module (600V FRED with 600V IGBT, 1200V FRED with 1200V IGBT). Current rating should be 50-100% of IGBT current depending on duty cycle. The soft recovery characteristic helps reduce EMI and switching losses in inverter applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match FRED ratings to your IGBT module for optimal performance.

products.application: PFC Boost, Output Rectifiers, Anti-parallel Diodes
products.shortDescription: MacMic MMF100J060D1 600V 100A FRED module with fast recovery. Ideal for PFC boost stages and output rectifiers in 400...
descriptionParagraphs: The MacMic MMF100J060D1 is a high-performance 600V 100A Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode module featuring fast recovery time of 35ns typical.
With low reverse recovery charge and soft recovery characteristics, this D1 package module minimizes switching losses and EMI in high-frequency rectification applications.
The 600V rating is ideal for PFC boost stages and output rectifiers in 400V AC systems, while the compact D1 package ensures easy integration.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMF100J060D1 is a Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode (FRED) module designed for high-frequency rectification applications. Featuring advanced epitaxial technology, this module achieves a fast recovery time of 35ns typical with low reverse recovery charge (Qrr), significantly reducing switching losses in power conversion systems. The soft recovery characteristic minimizes voltage overshoot and EMI, improving system reliability. The 600V voltage rating is well-suited for PFC boost stages and output rectifiers in 400V AC input systems. The 100A current rating supports medium-power applications, and the module can be used as an anti-parallel diode for 600V IGBT modules. The D1 package provides compact size with good thermal performance.

faeReview.author: Lisa Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Conversion
faeReview.content: In my 8 years working with PFC and rectifier designs, the MMF100J060D1 has proven to be a reliable and cost-effective FRED solution. The 35ns recovery time is genuine - I've measured it in actual circuits and it performs as specified. The soft recovery characteristic is particularly important as it reduces EMI and voltage overshoot, which can be a major issue with snap-off diodes. One practical tip: ensure proper thermal design as FREDs can run hot in continuous conduction mode. I typically recommend keeping the case temperature below 100°C for long-term reliability. For PFC applications, this diode pairs excellently with MacMic's 600V IGBT modules.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 35ns recovery with soft recovery characteristic reduces EMI and switching losses

faqs.question: How does MMF100J060D1 compare to other microcontrollers in the same class?
faqs.answer: Compared to competitors, MMF100J060D1 offers superior price-performance ratio with its ARM core running at . The comprehensive peripheral set including Fast recovery time: 35ns typical, Low reverse recovery charge (Qrr), Soft recovery characteristic provides flexibility for various applications. MacMic Science & Technology's mature ecosystem including development tools, extensive code libraries, and strong technical support significantly reduces development time. faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for MMF100J060D1?
faqs.answer: For optimal performance with MMF100J060D1: (1) Power supply - place decoupling capacitors (100nF ceramic + 4.7µF) close to each power pin. Use proper bulk capacitance for stable operation. (2) Clock circuit - keep crystal oscillator traces short and away from high-speed signals. (3) High-speed signals - route USB, Ethernet, and other high-speed interfaces with proper impedance matching. Keep them away from analog traces. (4) Grounding - implement solid ground planes and minimize ground loops. (5) Thermal management - ensure adequate copper area for heat dissipation, especially for high-frequency operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our reference PCB layout guide or contact our FAE team for layout review services.

faqs.question: What are the recommended operating conditions for MMF100J060D1?
faqs.answer: MMF100J060D1 operates at 1.8V to 3.6V supply voltage, with 3.3V being the most common configuration. The operating temperature range is -40°C to +85°C. For reliable operation at , ensure stable power supply with low ripple. The Flash memory requires proper wait state configuration based on operating frequency. When using analog peripherals, use a separate analog supply with proper filtering. The maximum GPIO output current is typically 8-25mA per pin, with a total limit for all pins. Always refer to the datasheet for detailed electrical characteristics and derating curves.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete datasheet for detailed electrical characteristics or contact our FAE team for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What are common debugging issues with MMF100J060D1 and their solutions?
faqs.answer: Common MMF100J060D1 debugging challenges: (1) JTAG/SWD connection failures - often caused by incorrect BOOT pin configuration or conflicting GPIO remapping. Solution: Check BOOT pin states, verify SWD pin alternate function settings. (2) Clock issues - typically due to incorrect PLL configuration or crystal problems. Solution: Verify HSE crystal and PLL settings using debugging tools. (3) Peripheral initialization failures - usually related to incorrect clock enabling or configuration. Solution: Ensure proper clock tree configuration, check peripheral initialization sequence. (4) Power-related resets - may occur due to insufficient decoupling or voltage drops. Solution: Add adequate decoupling capacitors, check power supply stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for debugging assistance or check our online knowledge base for detailed troubleshooting guides.

faqs.question: How to select peripherals and configure MMF100J060D1 for my application?
faqs.answer: To configure MMF100J060D1 for your application: (1) List all required interfaces and processing requirements. (2) Use the manufacturer's configuration tool to set up peripherals and generate initialization code. (3) Key selection criteria: Communication interfaces based on device compatibility and speed requirements; Analog peripherals for sensor interfacing; Timers for PWM and timing functions; Memory resources for code and data. (4) Consider using DMA for high-throughput peripherals to reduce CPU overhead. (5) Enable only required peripherals to minimize power consumption. (6) Implement proper interrupt priority management for real-time response.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection tool or contact our FAE team for personalized configuration recommendations based on your project requirements.

With fast recovery time of 50ns and low reverse recovery charge, this D1 package module delivers efficient performance in output rectifiers and brake chopper circuits.

products.longDescription: The MacMic MMF200J120D1 is a high-current Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode (FRED) module designed for demanding rectification applications in industrial power systems. Featuring advanced epitaxial technology, this module provides a fast recovery time of 50ns with low reverse recovery charge, minimizing switching losses in high-frequency applications. The 1200V voltage rating is ideal for 690V AC industrial systems, while the 200A current rating supports high-power applications such as output rectifiers for motor drives, brake choppers for dynamic braking, and anti-parallel diodes for 1200V IGBT modules. The soft recovery characteristic reduces voltage overshoot and EMI, improving overall system reliability.

faeReview.author: Robert Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Drives
faeReview.content: In my 15 years supporting industrial drive applications, the MMF200J120D1 has become my preferred choice for output rectifiers in medium-power VFDs. The combination of 1200V rating and 200A current handles most 30-75kW drive applications very well. The 50ns recovery time is fast enough for typical 6-8KHz switching frequencies used in motor drives. One important consideration: in brake chopper applications, ensure adequate heat sinking as the diode can see high peak currents during braking. I typically recommend derating to 70% of rated current for continuous operation in brake choppers. The soft recovery is a real advantage - I've seen significantly less EMI issues compared to using standard ultrafast diodes.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 1200V 200A combination for industrial drives with soft recovery reducing EMI issues

faqs.answer: For optimal performance with MMF200J120D1: (1) Power supply - place decoupling capacitors (100nF ceramic + 4.7µF) close to each power pin. faqs.question: What are common debugging issues with MMF200J120D1 and their solutions?
faqs.answer: Common MMF200J120D1 debugging challenges: (1) JTAG/SWD connection failures - often caused by incorrect BOOT pin configuration or conflicting GPIO remapping. Solution: Check BOOT pin states, verify SWD pin alternate function settings. (2) Clock issues - typically due to incorrect PLL configuration or crystal problems. Solution: Verify HSE crystal and PLL settings using debugging tools. (3) Peripheral initialization failures - usually related to incorrect clock enabling or configuration. Solution: Ensure proper clock tree configuration, check peripheral initialization sequence. (4) Power-related resets - may occur due to insufficient decoupling or voltage drops. Solution: Add adequate decoupling capacitors, check power supply stability.

categories.name: Power Discrete Devices
categories.description: MacMic IGBT and FRED discrete devices in TO-220, TO-3P, and TO-247 packages for various power applications requiring through-hole mounting.
categories.longDescription: MacMic's power discrete device portfolio includes IGBT and FRED chips in industry-standard through-hole packages including TO-220, TO-3P, and TO-247. These discrete devices offer flexibility for applications where module packaging is not suitable, such as low-power designs, custom configurations, or replacement parts. The IGBT discrete devices feature the same Trench Field-Stop technology as the module products, providing consistent performance characteristics. FRED discrete devices offer fast recovery times and soft recovery characteristics for rectification applications. These devices are ideal for auxiliary power supplies, PFC stages, brake choppers, and low-power inverter applications. As your trusted MacMic power discrete distributor, we can help you choose the right package and ratings. Need MacMic discrete Selection guidance? Contact our FAE team or check our technical resources. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Discrete Devices selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select MacMic Power Discrete Devices
selectionGuide.description: Guide to choosing the right discrete package and ratings for your power application
keyConsiderations: Package selection: TO-220 for low power, TO-247 for higher power
Voltage rating: Match to DC bus voltage with margin
Current rating: Include thermal limitations in selection
Thermal design: Discrete packages require careful heat sink design
Mounting: Through-hole for reliability, isolation requirements

faqs.question: When should I use discrete devices instead of modules?
faqs.answer: Use discrete devices when: (1) Power requirements are lower (< 50A continuous); (2) Custom thermal solutions are needed; (3) Space constraints favor single devices; (4) Cost optimization for high-volume production; (5) Replacement of existing discrete designs. TO-247 packages offer good thermal performance up to 100A with proper heat sinking. Modules are preferred for higher power and integrated half-bridge configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider power level, thermal design, and system integration requirements when choosing between discrete and module packages.

faqs.question: What is the difference between TO-220 and TO-247 packages?
faqs.answer: TO-220 is a smaller package suitable for currents up to 20-30A with appropriate heat sinking. It has lower thermal mass and is ideal for low-power applications. TO-247 is larger with better thermal characteristics, supporting currents up to 100A. TO-247 has lower thermal resistance (Rth) and can handle higher power dissipation. Both packages use through-hole mounting for reliable mechanical connection. Choose TO-220 for compact designs, TO-247 for higher power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select package based on current requirements and available heat sink size.

faqs.question: How do I properly heat sink discrete power devices?
faqs.answer: Proper thermal design for discrete devices includes: (1) Use thermal interface material (TIM) with conductivity >3 W/mK; (2) Ensure mounting torque of 0.8-1.2 Nm for good thermal contact; (3) Design heat sink with thermal resistance (Rth) appropriate for your power dissipation; (4) Consider forced air cooling for continuous operation above 50A; (5) Keep case temperature below 100°C for long-term reliability. Calculate junction temperature using Tj = Ta + Ploss × (Rth(j-c) + Rth(c-s) + Rth(s-a)).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal design assistance and heat sink recommendations.

products.application: Auxiliary Power Supplies, PFC Boost, SMPS
products.shortDescription: MacMic MMG30J060U1 600V 30A IGBT discrete in TO-247 package. Ideal for auxiliary power supplies and PFC applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MacMic MMG30J060U1 is a 600V 30A IGBT discrete device in industry-standard TO-247 package, featuring Trench Field-Stop technology.
With Vce(sat) of 1.8V typical and robust TO-247 package, this device is well-suited for auxiliary power supplies, PFC boost stages, and low-power inverter applications.
The through-hole TO-247 package provides excellent thermal performance and reliable mounting for industrial applications.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMG30J060U1 is a 600V 30A IGBT discrete device packaged in the industry-standard TO-247 through-hole package. Utilizing Trench Field-Stop technology, this device offers low conduction losses with typical Vce(sat) of 1.8V and reliable switching performance. The 600V voltage rating is ideal for 400V AC input applications, while the 30A current rating supports various auxiliary power and PFC applications. The TO-247 package provides excellent thermal characteristics when properly heat-sinked, allowing reliable continuous operation. This device is commonly used in auxiliary power supplies for motor drives, PFC boost stages, brake chopper circuits, and low-power SMPS applications. The through-hole mounting ensures reliable mechanical connection and good thermal contact.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The MMG30J060U1 is my go-to recommendation for auxiliary power supplies in motor drive systems. The TO-247 package is reliable and easy to work with. Key insight: don't underestimate the thermal design - while TO-247 has good thermal characteristics, you still need proper heat sinking for continuous operation at rated current. I typically recommend keeping case temperature below 90°C for long-term reliability. The 30A rating is realistic with good thermal design. For PFC applications, this device works well at switching frequencies up to 50KHz with proper gate drive. One practical tip: use a good thermal interface material and ensure adequate mounting torque (0.8-1.2 Nm typical) for optimal thermal contact.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable TO-247 package with genuine 30A capability when properly heat-sinked

products.longDescription: The MacMic MMF75J120U1 is a high-current Fast Recovery Epitaxial Diode (FRED) in industry-standard TO-247 package. Designed for high-frequency rectification applications, this device features a fast recovery time of 50ns with low reverse recovery charge (Qrr), significantly reducing switching losses. The soft recovery characteristic minimizes voltage overshoot and EMI compared to standard ultrafast diodes. The 1200V voltage rating is ideal for 690V AC industrial systems, while the 75A current rating supports various rectification applications including output rectifiers for motor drives, brake chopper circuits, and anti-parallel diodes for IGBT discrete devices. The TO-247 package provides excellent thermal performance and reliable through-hole mounting.

categories.name: MOSFET Modules
categories.description: MacMic low on-resistance MOSFET modules for high-power synchronous rectification and switching applications.
categories.longDescription: MacMic's MOSFET module portfolio features low on-resistance devices designed for high-power synchronous rectification and switching applications. These modules offer excellent switching performance with low gate charge and fast switching times, making them ideal for high-frequency applications where IGBT switching losses would be prohibitive. The low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses, improving overall system efficiency. Available in various voltage and current ratings, these MOSFET modules are well-suited for synchronous rectifiers in power supplies, DC-DC converters, and high-frequency inverters. The module packaging provides excellent thermal performance and reliable mounting for industrial applications. As your trusted MacMic MOSFET distributor, we provide technical support for selecting the optimal MOSFET for your synchronous rectification needs. Need MacMic MOSFET Selection guidance? Contact our FAE team. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive MOSFET Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.
categories.seoKeywords: MacMic MOSFET, power MOSFET module, synchronous rectification, low RDS(on), MacMic distributor

selectionGuide.title: How to Select MacMic MOSFET Modules
selectionGuide.description: Guide to choosing MOSFET modules based on voltage, current, and switching requirements
keyConsiderations: Voltage rating: Consider derating for reliable operation
RDS(on): Lower for reduced conduction losses
Gate charge: Lower for faster switching
Switching frequency: MOSFETs excel at high frequencies
Thermal design: Ensure adequate heat sinking

faqs.question: When should I choose MOSFET over IGBT for my application?
faqs.answer: Choose MOSFET over IGBT when: (1) Operating voltage is below 200V (MOSFETs have lower conduction losses at low voltages); (2) Switching frequency is above 100KHz (MOSFETs switch faster with lower switching losses); (3) Synchronous rectification is needed (MOSFETs can conduct in reverse with low voltage drop); (4) Application requires 12V, 24V, or 48V output (server power, telecom, battery systems). IGBTs are preferred for higher voltages (>300V) and lower frequencies (<20KHz).
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage level and switching frequency when choosing between MOSFET and IGBT.

faqs.question: How does RDS(on) affect my power supply efficiency?
faqs.answer: RDS(on) directly determines conduction losses in MOSFETs. Conduction loss = I² × RDS(on) × duty cycle. Lower RDS(on) means higher efficiency but typically higher cost and larger die size. For example, reducing RDS(on) from 5mΩ to 2.5mΩ halves conduction losses at the same current. In high-current applications (100A+), even small RDS(on) differences significantly impact efficiency and thermal design. Balance cost, efficiency, and thermal requirements when selecting RDS(on).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses based on your current requirements and efficiency targets.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements do MacMic MOSFET modules have?
faqs.answer: MacMic MOSFET modules require: (1) Gate voltage of 10-12V for full enhancement (never exceed 20V); (2) Gate driver with sufficient current capability (2A+ for fast switching); (3) Proper gate resistor selection (typically 5-15Ω) to control switching speed and EMI; (4) Miller clamp protection to prevent false turn-on during high dv/dt transitions; (5) Fast switching requires low inductance gate drive loop. Use dedicated synchronous rectifier controllers for optimal performance in rectification applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your gate driver can provide adequate current and voltage for the MOSFET module.

products.shortDescription: MacMic MMO100N060Y1 60V 100A MOSFET module with ultra-low 2.5mΩ RDS(on). Ideal for synchronous rectification in high-...
descriptionParagraphs: The MacMic MMO100N060Y1 is a high-performance 60V 100A MOSFET module featuring ultra-low RDS(on) of 2.5mΩ typical, designed for high-efficiency synchronous rectification.
With low gate charge and fast switching characteristics, this Y1 package module delivers excellent performance in high-frequency DC-DC converters and SMPS applications.
The compact Y1 package provides excellent thermal performance and is optimized for synchronous rectifier applications requiring high current with minimal losses.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMO100N060Y1 is a high-current MOSFET module designed specifically for synchronous rectification applications. Featuring ultra-low RDS(on) of 2.5mΩ typical, this module minimizes conduction losses in high-current power supplies. The 60V voltage rating is ideal for 12V, 24V, and 48V output rectifiers, while the 100A current rating supports high-power applications. Low gate charge enables fast switching with minimal drive power requirements. The Y1 package is optimized for synchronous rectifier applications, providing excellent thermal performance and low parasitic inductance. This module is particularly well-suited for server power supplies, telecom rectifiers, and high-current DC-DC converters where efficiency is critical.
features: Low gate charge for fast switching

faeReview.author: Michael Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Supplies
faeReview.content: The MMO100N060Y1 is an excellent choice for high-current synchronous rectification. The 2.5mΩ RDS(on) is genuinely low - I've measured it in actual applications and it delivers the expected efficiency improvements. In a 48V/50A server power supply design, replacing traditional diode rectifiers with these MOSFETs improved efficiency by 3-4%. Key insight: gate drive design is critical for synchronous rectifiers - you need fast turn-on and precise timing to avoid shoot-through. I recommend using dedicated synchronous rectifier controllers with proper dead-time control. Thermal design is also important - while the Y1 package has good thermal characteristics, at 100A continuous you need proper heat sinking. I typically see 40-50W conduction losses at full load, so plan your thermal design accordingly.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low 2.5mΩ RDS(on) enables 3-4% efficiency improvement in high-current rectifiers

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current with 28% lower RDS(on) for highest efficiency applications
Higher voltage rating for applications requiring more voltage margin

The Y1 package provides excellent thermal performance. The low gate charge enables high-frequency operation, allowing smaller passive components. Common applications include: synchronous rectification, motor control, battery management, and switching power supplies.

faqs.answer: MMO100N060Y1 stands out with its low RDS(on) of 2.5mΩ (typ), which is competitive in its voltage class. This translates to lower conduction losses and higher efficiency. The gate charge of  enables efficient high-frequency switching. MacMic Science & Technology's technology provides excellent Figure of Merit (FOM). The package offers superior thermal performance compared to standard packages. faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for MMO100N060Y1?
faqs.answer: For optimal MMO100N060Y1 performance: (1) Thermal management - use adequate copper area with thermal vias connecting to inner planes. This is critical for heat dissipation. (2) Gate drive - keep gate traces short and wide to minimize inductance. Use Kelvin connections for source sensing in high-current applications. (3) Decoupling - place ceramic capacitors close to the gate driver IC. (4) Current sensing - place sense resistor close to the source pin with dedicated traces. (5) Layout symmetry - maintain symmetrical layout in bridge configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our reference PCB layout guide or contact our FAE team for layout review and thermal simulation support.

faqs.question: What are the recommended operating conditions for MMO100N060Y1?
faqs.answer: MMO100N060Y1 operates as a 60V MOSFET with continuous drain current up to 100A at 25°C case temperature. The gate threshold voltage is typically 2-4V, with recommended gate drive voltage of 10V for full enhancement. The maximum junction temperature is 175°C, but for reliable operation, maintain Tj below 125°C. RDS(on) increases with temperature. The maximum pulsed drain current is typically 3-4 times the continuous rating. Always include safety margins in your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete datasheet for detailed electrical characteristics or contact our FAE team for application-specific derating recommendations.

faqs.question: What are common design issues with MMO100N060Y1 and their solutions?
faqs.answer: Common MMO100N060Y1 design challenges: (1) Thermal runaway at high currents - caused by insufficient heat sinking. Solution: Implement proper thermal vias, use large copper pours. (2) Gate oscillations - due to long gate traces or inadequate gate resistance. Solution: Keep gate traces short, add appropriate gate resistor. (3) Voltage spikes during switching - caused by parasitic inductance. Solution: Minimize loop inductance, add snubber circuits if necessary. (4) False triggering in high-side applications - due to dV/dt. Solution: Use negative gate drive, add Miller clamp circuit.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for design review services or access our application notes library.

faqs.question: How to select gate driver and design gate drive circuit for MMO100N060Y1?
faqs.answer: For optimal MMO100N060Y1 performance, gate driver selection is critical: (1) Drive capability - choose a driver that can provide sufficient peak current to charge/discharge the gate charge quickly. (2) UVLO protection - ensure the driver has undervoltage lockout. (3) Dead-time control - in bridge applications, implement adequate dead-time to prevent shoot-through. (4) Bootstrap circuit - for high-side drivers, use proper bootstrap components. (5) Gate resistor - select appropriate value to control switching speed and reduce EMI. (6) Decoupling - place capacitors close to the driver supply pins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver recommendations or request reference designs for your specific topology.

products.application: High-Current Synchronous Rectification, Battery Charging, Welding Supplies
products.shortDescription: MacMic MMO200N040Y1 40V 200A MOSFET module with ultra-low 1.2mΩ RDS(on). Designed for ultra-high current synchronous ...
descriptionParagraphs: The MacMic MMO200N040Y1 is an ultra-high current 40V 200A MOSFET module featuring industry-leading RDS(on) of 1.2mΩ typical.
Designed for the most demanding synchronous rectification applications, this Y1 package module delivers exceptional efficiency in high-current power supplies and battery charging systems.
The 40V rating is optimized for 12V and 24V output applications, while the 200A current capability supports the highest power density designs.
products.longDescription: The MacMic MMO200N040Y1 represents the pinnacle of MOSFET module technology for synchronous rectification. With ultra-low RDS(on) of just 1.2mΩ typical and 200A current rating, this module enables unprecedented efficiency in high-current power conversion applications. The 40V voltage rating is specifically optimized for 12V and 24V output rectifiers commonly found in server power supplies, battery charging systems, and welding equipment. Advanced chip technology minimizes both conduction and switching losses, while the Y1 package provides excellent thermal performance for continuous high-current operation. This module is ideal for applications requiring the highest power density and efficiency, such as data center power supplies, industrial battery chargers, and high-current welding power sources.
features: Low gate charge for efficient switching

faeReview.author: Robert Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The MMO200N040Y1 is simply outstanding for high-current applications. The 1.2mΩ RDS(on) is the lowest I've seen in this package size. In a 12V/200A industrial battery charger design, we achieved 98.5% rectifier efficiency using these modules - that's 2-3% better than competing solutions. At 200A, conduction losses are about 48W, so thermal design is critical. I recommend forced air cooling or liquid cooling for continuous operation at full current. Gate drive requirements are significant at 120nC gate charge - use a 4A+ gate driver for fast switching. One important consideration: layout is critical at these currents. Minimize parasitic inductance in the source path to avoid voltage spikes during switching. Use wide, short traces and proper decoupling.
faeReview.highlight: Industry-leading 1.2mΩ RDS(on) enables 98.5% rectifier efficiency in high-current applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher voltage rating for applications requiring more voltage margin, lower current
Ultra-high current with even lower RDS(on) for extreme power applications

faqs.question: What are the main application scenarios for MMO200N040Y1?
faqs.answer: MMO200N040Y1 excels in high-frequency switching applications. Its 40V voltage rating and low RDS(on) of 1.2mΩ (typ) make it ideal for DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power supplies. The Y1 package provides excellent thermal performance. The low gate charge enables high-frequency operation, allowing smaller passive components. Common applications include: synchronous rectification, motor control, battery management, and switching power supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to evaluate MMO200N040Y1 for your switching application and receive thermal design recommendations.

faqs.question: How does MMO200N040Y1 compare to competing MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: MMO200N040Y1 stands out with its low RDS(on) of 1.2mΩ (typ), which is competitive in its voltage class. This translates to lower conduction losses and higher efficiency. The gate charge of  enables efficient high-frequency switching. MacMic Science & Technology's technology provides excellent Figure of Merit (FOM). The package offers superior thermal performance compared to standard packages. Additionally, MacMic Science & Technology's manufacturing quality and long-term availability commitment provide supply chain security.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a detailed comparison report including efficiency calculations for your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: What are the key PCB layout considerations for MMO200N040Y1?
faqs.answer: For optimal MMO200N040Y1 performance: (1) Thermal management - use adequate copper area with thermal vias connecting to inner planes. This is critical for heat dissipation. (2) Gate drive - keep gate traces short and wide to minimize inductance. Use Kelvin connections for source sensing in high-current applications. (3) Decoupling - place ceramic capacitors close to the gate driver IC. (4) Current sensing - place sense resistor close to the source pin with dedicated traces. (5) Layout symmetry - maintain symmetrical layout in bridge configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download our reference PCB layout guide or contact our FAE team for layout review and thermal simulation support.

faqs.question: What are the recommended operating conditions for MMO200N040Y1?
faqs.answer: MMO200N040Y1 operates as a 40V MOSFET with continuous drain current up to 200A at 25°C case temperature. The gate threshold voltage is typically 2-4V, with recommended gate drive voltage of 10V for full enhancement. The maximum junction temperature is 175°C, but for reliable operation, maintain Tj below 125°C. RDS(on) increases with temperature. The maximum pulsed drain current is typically 3-4 times the continuous rating. Always include safety margins in your design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete datasheet for detailed electrical characteristics or contact our FAE team for application-specific derating recommendations.

faqs.question: What are common design issues with MMO200N040Y1 and their solutions?
faqs.answer: Common MMO200N040Y1 design challenges: (1) Thermal runaway at high currents - caused by insufficient heat sinking. Solution: Implement proper thermal vias, use large copper pours. (2) Gate oscillations - due to long gate traces or inadequate gate resistance. Solution: Keep gate traces short, add appropriate gate resistor. (3) Voltage spikes during switching - caused by parasitic inductance. Solution: Minimize loop inductance, add snubber circuits if necessary. (4) False triggering in high-side applications - due to dV/dt. Solution: Use negative gate drive, add Miller clamp circuit.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for design review services or access our application notes library.
